content.problemStatement:
Chef has an array AAA of length NNN. He can perform the following operation on AAA:

**1)** Select an index iii (1≤i≤N−1)(1 \le i \le N - 1)(1≤i≤N−1) and select an integer XXX (1≤X<220)(1 \le X \lt 2^{20})(1≤X<220). \\
**2)** Set Ai:=(Ai⊕X)A_i := (A_i \oplus X)Ai​:=(Ai​⊕X) and Ai+1:=(Ai+1⊕X)A_{i + 1} := (A_{i + 1} \oplus X)Ai+1​:=(Ai+1​⊕X). (Here, ⊕\oplus⊕ denotes the bitwise XOR operation)

Determine if Chef can make all the elements of AAA equal by applying the above operation any number of times (possibly zero).


Input Format

The first line contains a single integer TTT — the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
The first line of each test case contains an integer NNN — the size of the array AAA.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \dots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​ denoting the array AAA.


Output Format
For each test case, output `YES` if it is possible to make all the elements of AAA equal by applying the given operation any number of times. Otherwise, output `NO`.


You may print each character of `YES` and `NO` in uppercase or lowercase (for example, `yes`, `yEs`, `Yes` will be considered identical).
constraints: The sum of N over all test cases does not exceed 2·10^5
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines: the first line contains an integer N, the size of the array. The second line contains N space‑separated integers A1, A2, …, AN.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing "YES" if it is possible to make all array elements equal using the allowed operations, otherwise output "NO".

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: N is even and total XOR = 0, so YES.\nTest case 2: N is even but total XOR = 1 ≠ 0, so NO.\nTest case 3: N is odd, always YES.

solution.approach:
The operation XORs the same value X to two adjacent elements. This does not change the XOR of the whole array because X⊕X = 0. Therefore the total XOR of the array is an invariant. If after all operations every element equals some value V, then the total XOR becomes:
- V if N is odd (odd number of V's)
- 0 if N is even.
Hence:
* If N is odd, any initial total XOR can be turned into V = (total XOR), so the answer is always YES.
* If N is even, we need the invariant total XOR to be 0; otherwise it is impossible.
The algorithm simply computes the XOR of the array and applies the above rule.
Time complexity is O(N) per test case, overall O(total N). Memory usage is O(1) besides input storage.
solution.timeComplexity: O(total N) where total N is the sum of N over all test cases (≤ 2·10^5).
